alt: A small robot on a luminous timeline raising a lantern to glimpse the next upcoming event while earlier events dim behind it, capturing temporal intelligence as reasoning across past and future along the axis of time.
caption: Temporal intelligence is the craft of using everything that already happened to illuminate the one thing that has not happened yet.

after_heading: Time as the Hidden Axis of Intelligence
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tidy pile of identical shuffled cards floating freely on the left versus the same cards on the right snapped firmly onto a single glowing arrow that runs left to right, each card now leaning on the one before it like dominoes. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: On the left, interchangeable data cards float in a loose unordered pile; on the right the same cards are threaded onto a single time arrow, each leaning on its predecessor, showing how order turns independent samples into a dependent sequence.
caption: Drop the i.i.d. assumption and the data stops being a bag of interchangeable points; it becomes a chain where each moment leans on the one before it.

after_heading: Memory and Horizon: How Far Back, How Far Forward
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cheerful robot stands at the center of a long road, one hand cupped behind its head to listen back down the road it came from while the other hand holds a small telescope aimed forward into a softening haze, the road behind crisp and the road ahead dissolving into fog. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive pose, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot on a road looks back over a clear stretch of past while peering forward through a telescope into thickening fog, dramatizing memory as how far back it can see and horizon as how far forward it dares to predict.
caption: Every temporal model lives between two dials: how much past it remembers and how far into the fog ahead it is willing to forecast.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a beaming overconfident forecaster on a podium proudly points at a wildly wiggly curve that threads perfectly through every past dot, while just past the edge of the curve the real next dot veers off in a completely different direction behind the forecaster's back. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, dry knowing humor, expressive face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A proud forecaster on a podium points at an overfit squiggly curve that hits every historical point perfectly, oblivious to the actual next data point veering off in another direction, illustrating overfitting and false confidence about the future.
caption: A curve that explains every past wiggle perfectly is usually most confident exactly where it is about to be most wrong.

after_heading: Decision Making: Acting to Shape the Future
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a curious robot stands at a fork of glowing branching paths, having already taken a few small footsteps down one branch, peeking eagerly down an untried branch with a hopeful sparkle in its eyes, its footprints feeding back as a soft glow that brightens the path it chose. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive curious pose, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A curious robot at a fork of glowing paths has stepped partway down one branch while eyeing an untried branch, with its own footprints feeding back to brighten the chosen path, depicting an agent acting to shape future outcomes and balancing exploration against exploitation.
caption: Decision making closes the loop: an agent does not just predict the future, it steps into it and changes which future arrives.

after_heading: The Predictability Ceiling: Bounds No Model Can Beat
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a stack of increasingly fancy robots forms a wobbly cheerleader pyramid all straining upward, fingertips of the top robot stopping just short of a smooth glowing horizontal glass ceiling above them, beyond which swirls a region of pure random sparkles that cannot be reached. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, gentle humor, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A pyramid of ever more elaborate robots strains upward but the topmost fingertips stop just below a smooth glowing ceiling, above which lies an unreachable region of pure random sparkles, illustrating an irreducible predictability ceiling no model can beat.
caption: Past a certain point the limit is the data, not the model: no amount of cleverness pushes prediction through the ceiling of irreducible noise.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a weary seen-it-all robot detective stands confidently on a patterned floor tile aiming a magnifying glass at where a target used to be, while behind its back the entire floor of tiles is silently sliding and rearranging into a different layout, leaving its careful aim pointing at empty space. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, dry knowing humor, expressive resigned face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A world-weary robot detective aims a magnifying glass with confidence while the tiled floor beneath and behind it silently slides into a new arrangement, leaving its careful aim pointing at empty space, dramatizing concept drift as the ground shifting under a trained model.
caption: Concept drift is the quiet betrayal of temporal modeling: the rules you learned keep rearranging themselves the moment you stop watching.

after_heading: The Temporal Thread: Classical Ideas Returning in Learned Form
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a gentle spiral staircase where weathered old-fashioned brass gears and clockwork pieces at the bottom steps gradually transform, step by step rising upward, into the same shapes rebuilt out of glowing modern neural-network nodes and threads at the top, the silhouette identical at bottom and top. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A spiral staircase whose lowest steps are weathered brass clockwork gears that transform step by step into the same shapes rebuilt from glowing neural network nodes at the top, showing classical temporal methods returning in learned neural form.
caption: The same classical ideas keep climbing back into view, rebuilt in learned form: the machinery changes but the temporal shape underneath stays the same.
